summary: Checkout has a ~31% drop-off between add-to-cart and order-placed, and reducing it is the priority for Q3.

provenance.quote: We're at something like a 31% drop-off between "add to cart" and "order placed."
So this revamp is the priority for Q3.

summary: The goal is to reduce checkout drop-off from 31% to under 20% this release.

provenance.quote: The number we floated was getting it from 31% down to under 20%.
Cart → order-placed drop-off is ~31%. Target: under 20% this release.

summary: Most of the checkout drop-off is concentrated at the payment step.

provenance.quote: Most of the pain is concentrated at the payment step (per support tickets + the funnel data).

summary: Guest checkout is a must-have: a user with an item in the cart should reach 'order placed' without ever creating an account.

provenance.quote: Agreed, guest checkout is a must-have. If you have an item in your cart you should be able to get to "order placed" without ever creating an account.
Let people pay without signing up.

summary: The checkout should collapse to a single-page flow: cart summary, delivery address, payment, place order.

provenance.quote: Ideally one screen. Cart summary, delivery address, payment, place order. The fewer steps the better.
New single-page checkout: cart summary → delivery address → payment → place order.

summary: Payment must support Apple Pay and Google Pay alongside the existing manual card form.

provenance.quote: We need the wallets. Apple Pay, Google Pay.
Payment: add Apple Pay and Google Pay alongside the existing card form.

summary: On mobile, wallets should be the primary, most-prominent payment option.

provenance.quote: Wallets should be the primary, most-prominent option on mobile.

summary: Saved-card behavior (whether/how they work and how they interact with guest checkout) was raised and explicitly deferred as open/parked.

provenance.quote: Let's — yeah, let's come back to the saved cards thing, I want to think about how that interacts with guest. Dani, can you note that as open.
Saved cards — how they behave, where they show. (Parked from kickoff.)

summary: Every order must be tied to a Forkful account; the checkout will require the user to be signed in before placing an order.

provenance.quote:
Every order must be tied to a
  Forkful account

the checkout will require the user to be signed in before
  placing an order.

summary: Declined-payment handling is a known pain point today (no message on decline, users retry and rage-quit) and the new flow must feel less broken.

provenance.quote: when a card gets declined right now, the user just sees the form again with no message. They retry the same card three times and rage-quit. Whatever we build, that has to feel less broken.

summary: After placing the order the user lands on a confirmation screen (order number, estimated delivery time, 'we're on it'), described as the payoff.

provenance.quote: after they place the order, they land on a confirmation screen — order number, estimated delivery time, "we're on it." That moment matters, it's the payoff.

summary: Promo / discount codes (e.g. 'first order 20% off' campaigns) must work in the new flow.

provenance.quote: marketing's going to want promo codes to work in the new flow, they run a lot of "first order 20% off" campaigns.
Marketing runs "first order 20% off" promos; the new flow needs to not break those.

summary: Cart recovery / abandoned-cart email is explicitly parked and out of scope for this release.

provenance.quote: Yeah let's keep the cart-recovery stuff parked, this release is the checkout flow.
Cart recovery / abandoned-cart email — explicitly parked, not this release.

summary: In-session cart behavior was raised ('we don't lose the cart') but capped with 'let's not boil the ocean,' focusing on the checkout itself.

provenance.quote: What about people who add stuff to cart and leave?
I mean, ideally we don't lose the cart. But let's not boil the ocean. Focus on the checkout itself.

summary: Delivery ETA is shown on the cart summary and recalculated when the address changes.

provenance.quote: Delivery ETA shown on the cart summary, recalculated when the address changes.

summary: Loyalty points accrue on every completed order at 1 point per dollar, surfaced after the order completes.

provenance.quote:
Points accrue on every completed order — 1 point per dollar. Surface the points earned somewhere
  after the order completes.

summary: Design references cover the happy-path frames: cart summary, delivery address, payment (wallets), payment (card form), and place-order CTA state.

provenance.quote: | Cart summary | `…/FKFL-checkout-revamp?node=12:001` | Items, subtotal, delivery ETA, edit qty |
| Place order (CTA state) | `…/FKFL-checkout-revamp?node=12:045` | Loading / spinner on submit |

summary: Only the happy path is mocked so far; the back half of the flow is not yet mocked.

provenance.quote: Not yet mocked: still working through the back half of the flow. Will add frames as they're ready.
First pass covers the happy path we agreed on.
